title: Lineage of the RatisRav three-prose-piece selection
summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of a 1997 University of Michigan HTI electronic selection containing three independent Older Scots prose works: Craft of Deyng, Dicta Salomonis, and The Vertewis of the Mess. U-M OCRed and marked up selected pages from Greenwood Press's 1969 reprint of J. Rawson Lumby's 1870 EETS OS 43 edition; the call number and encoded title leaves establish the 1969 carrier even though the header bibliographic source statement gives the original 1870 imprint. Lumby worked from W. W. King's complete manuscript copy of Cambridge University Library MS Kk.1.5 part 6. That part is a separately covered historical component inside the current composite Kk.1.5 host and carries the selected works at ff. 1r-4r, 5r-12r, and 53r-54r. U-M separately consulted an unidentified manuscript microfilm to place folio milestones in Dicta Salomonis. Complete provider objects are cached for the compound Internet Archive scan containing Lumby, Girvan's complete 1939 edition, CUL's complete 172-canvas Ff.5.45 analogue manuscript, and the official CUL descriptions of Kk.1.5 and Ff.5.45. No complete public Kk.1.5 facsimile was found; official onsite and whole-object imaging-order routes are recorded. Ff.5.45 and Wynkyn de Worde's Vertue of the Masse are supporting analogues only, not ancestors of the selected texts.

W. W. King

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for RatisRav
entities.description: The pinned XML artifact consumed by this repository and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: File size is 60,783 bytes at the reviewed commit.

entities.label: University of Michigan HTI electronic item RatisRav (1997)
entities.description: OCR-derived electronic selection of only the three prose pieces in the 1969 Greenwood reprint. ScanWorx performed OCR; Janet Erickson marked up the text, Sean Pollack proofread it, and Jason Chu reviewed the markup. U-M omitted most print front matter and all editorial notes, while retaining a title-page representation and manuscript folio milestones.

entities.label: Greenwood Press reprint of Lumby's Ratis Raving (1969)
entities.description: The exact print carrier scanned and OCRed by U-M. The XML's encoded title leaves state First Greenwood Reprinting, 1969, and its source call number ends in 1969; this carrier is distinct from the original 1870 edition it reproduces.

bibliographic.citation: J. Rawson Lumby, ed., Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse (New York: Greenwood Press, 1969), reprint of EETS Original Series 43 (1870).
bibliographic.title: Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 43

entities.label: J. Rawson Lumby, Ratis Raving (1870)
entities.description: The original EETS OS 43 edition of all eleven works in Kk.1.5 part 6. The U-M selection uses item 1 at pp. 1-8, item 6 at pp. 11-25, and item 11 at pp. 113-115.

bibliographic.citation: J. Rawson Lumby, ed., Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, EETS Original Series 43 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1870).
bibliographic.title: Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 43

notes: Lumby records that W. W. King copied the entire manuscript section; that working transcript is modeled separately rather than silently collapsed into the manuscript witness.

entities.label: W. W. King's working transcript of Kk.1.5 part 6
entities.description: The complete manuscript copy made by King and used in preparing Lumby's edition. No present holding, shelfmark, or public copy was established.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Kk.1.5
entities.description: The current composite host: a bulky fifteenth-century paper codex of eight separately enumerated components, several now in separate covers. The selected works belong only to part 6.

notes: A number of hands; old brown calf binding; several components are now separately covered.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Kk.1.5, part 6
entities.description: The historically independent and separately covered paper component containing the eleven works edited by Lumby. Craft of Deyng is at ff. 1r-4r, Dicta Salomonis at ff. 5r-12r, and The Vertewis of the Mess at ff. 53r-54r.

holding.notes: Part of the present Kk.1.5 composite host; separately covered and historically independent before joining the neighboring Lancelot component.

physical_description.extent: 55 or 56 leaves in the checked descriptions
notes: James counts 56 leaves; Girvan describes 55. The discrepancy is preserved rather than silently normalized.
Approximately 38-39 lines per page in the selected section.

entities.label: Manuscript microfilm consulted by U-M for RatisRav
entities.description: An unidentified microfilm of the original manuscript consulted only to position folio milestone tags in Wisdom of Solomon / Dicta Salomonis when Lumby's print markers were ambiguous.

entities.label: Complete Kk.1.5 part 6 rotograph used by R. Girvan
entities.description: The complete manuscript rotograph that Girvan explicitly identifies as his working surrogate for Kk.1.5 part 6. No current holding, identifier, or public copy was found.

entities.label: R. Girvan, Ratis Raving and Other Early Scots Poems on Morals (1939)
entities.description: A later complete edition of Kk.1.5 part 6. Girvan prints the prose pieces in an appendix: Craft pp. 166-174, Dicta pp. 177-192, and Vertewis pp. 192-194, and supplies the principal component-level source and date analysis used here.

bibliographic.citation: R. Girvan, ed., Ratis Raving and Other Early Scots Poems on Morals, Scottish Text Society, 3rd series 11 (Edinburgh and London: William Blackwood & Sons, 1939).
bibliographic.title: Ratis Raving and Other Early Scots Poems on Morals

notes: Girvan's complete working rotograph is modeled as a separate unknown-survival facsimile intermediary.

entities.label: Internet Archive compound bound scan containing Lumby's 1870 edition
entities.description: A 528-page provider scan containing the complete Lumby volume followed by additional bound EETS material. It is complete as the delivered provider object but is not a standalone scan of only Lumby.
entities.reproduction_format: 528-page PDF with OCR, scan data, and metadata derivatives

entities.label: National Library of Scotland scan of Girvan's 1939 edition
entities.description: Complete 336-page provider PDF of Girvan's edition, including preliminary matter, text, notes, all seven appendix items, glossary, and society lists.

entities.label: CUL publication of M. R. James's historical Kk.1.5 description (2021)
entities.description: Official CUL publication of James's unfinished 1926-1930 description, in Martin Blake's 2002-2011 transcription. It is historical catalog evidence, not a claim to supersede all current codicological scholarship.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Ff.5.45
entities.description: A fifteenth-century devotional manuscript carrying a fuller Middle English Book of the Craft of Dying at ff. 24v-46r. Lumby and Girvan use it as comparison evidence for Craft of Deyng; no direct-exemplar relationship is established.

notes: The analogue Book of the Craft of Dying occupies ff. 24v-46r.

entities.label: CUL complete IIIF facsimile of MS Ff.5.45
entities.description: Complete 172-canvas provider object containing outer and inner covers, endleaves, every recto and verso of fols. 1-72, rear endleaves, spine, and three edge views.

entities.label: CUL publication of H. L. Pink's Ff.5.45 description (2021)
entities.description: Official CUL publication of Pink's handwritten description. It locates the fuller Craft of Dying analogue at ff. 24v-46r and describes its prologue and six parts.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde, The Vertue of the Masse (circa 1520)
entities.description: A short verse work attributed to John Lydgate and printed by Wynkyn de Worde, cited as a comparison for sayings in The Vertewis of the Mess. It is supporting analogue evidence, not a demonstrated source or carrier of the Scots prose tract.

bibliographic.citation: John Lydgate, The vertue of the masse ([London]: Wynkyn de Worde, [1520?]), STC 17038.
bibliographic.title: The vertue of the masse

access.access_method: Register and order MS Kk.1.5 for consultation in the CUL Special Collections Reading Room

access.access_method: CUDL search and the deterministic IIIF shelfmark endpoint were checked; no manifest or complete public image object for Kk.1.5 was exposed
notes: The expected endpoint https://cudl.lib.cam.ac.uk/iiif/MS-KK-00001-00005 returned an error while the adjacent shelfmark Kk.1.6 resolved under the same pattern; broad CUDL and web searches also found no Kk.1.5 object.

local_copies.label: Complete CUL MS Ff.5.45 IIIF bundle
notes: The complete bundle includes covers, all endleaves, fols. 1r-72v, spine, and three edge views at a non-upscaling 1600-pixel-wide IIIF derivative.

The fuller English Book of the Craft of Dying analogue is at ff. 24v-46r / canvases 60-103, but none of the three cataloged RatisRav works is present in this manuscript; the analogue range is therefore not a work_portion.

rights.component: Underlying fifteenth-century texts of the three selected works
rights.basis: The underlying texts and manuscript witness long predate modern copyright terms.

The embedded availability statement permits copying by individuals for personal use, research, and teaching if retained, permits linking, and expressly forbids redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers. It is not generalized to the medieval text, print editions, images, or repository layer.

rights.component: 1870 edition text and public provider scan
rights.basis: Published in 1870; Internet Archive supplies unrestricted public full-view access but no item-specific license field.

rights.component: 1969 reprint packaging and any new matter
rights.basis: The 1969 reprint is distinct from the public-domain 1870 edition and no item-level public reuse grant was found.

rights.component: Girvan's 1939 editorial matter and NLS scan
rights.basis: The volume states All Rights Reserved and the checked direct-file delivery supplies no item-level reuse license; public download does not itself establish redistribution permission.
The Apollo item applies Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.
The Apollo item applies Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: Any newly supplied CUL images of Kk.1.5
rights.basis: CUL imaging supply and reuse are subject to quotation, payment, stated terms, and permission for intended reuse; no images have been ordered for this record.

editorial_practices.source_feature: King's complete copy of Kk.1.5 part 6 and collateral comparison witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Edited print text with folio markers, notes, and comparison references
editorial_practices.description: Lumby's edition is mediated by King's working transcript. Its notes compare Craft with Ff.5.45 and Douce 322 and compare Vertewis sayings with related English material; those comparisons do not make the collateral sources ancestors.
editorial_practices.scope: The three selected prose works within the complete eleven-work edition

editorial_practices.source_feature: Greenwood's complete reprint of Lumby's prose-and-verse edition, including front matter, editorial notes, and eleven works
editorial_practices.representation: Three prose DIV1 units numbered 1, 6, and 11
editorial_practices.description: U-M explicitly included only the prose pieces and omitted the edition's editorial notes and most front matter. The electronic item's broad source title therefore does not define its intellectual boundary.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Ambiguous or absent folio markers around Lumby's Wisdom of Solomon text
editorial_practices.description: U-M consulted a microfilm of the original manuscript to position the Dicta Salomonis folio milestones. This affects structural tags, not the encoded wording's copytext.

editorial_practices.source_feature: All eleven works in Kk.1.5 part 6
editorial_practices.representation: Verse works in the main text and prose works in an appendix
editorial_practices.description: Girvan decided to print the whole manuscript part but relegated the prose pieces to the appendix because the main edition was already advanced.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete 1939 edition; target prose at pp. 166-194

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME source XML, RatisRav.xml.
evidence.summary: Establishes the 1997 electronic item, exact source call number and Greenwood title leaves, production workflow, prose-only selection, omitted material, microfilm consultation, three texts, print pagination, manuscript foliation, and legacy availability statement.

evidence.citation: Direct structural and fixity inspection of the pinned RatisRav XML.
evidence.summary: Confirms git blob cc64d7e4dbe1d403a0d0c55dc175c5cc09fe4405, 60,783 bytes, three BODY DIV1 units, 24 BODY P elements distributed 1, 1, and 22 by component, eight HEADER P elements and therefore 32 P elements file-wide, no L elements, and exact page and folio milestone ranges.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, RatisRav.
evidence.summary: Public item table of contents and structured text for the three electronic divisions.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 22582, Greenwood Press reprint of Ratis Raving.
evidence.summary: Confirms New York: Greenwood Press [1969], xii + 139 pages, EETS OS 43, and the note Reprint of the 1870 ed.

evidence.citation: J. Rawson Lumby, ed., Ratis Raving, and Other Moral and Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse, EETS OS 43 (1870).
evidence.locator: Title and preface at PDF pp. 9-12; Craft PDF pp. 19-26; Dicta PDF pp. 29-43; Vertewis PDF pp. 131-133; notes
evidence.summary: Confirms the original edition, King's transcript, Kk.1.5 part and folios, selected printed texts, and supporting comparisons. The complete 528-page compound provider PDF is cached.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata and scan-data derivatives for ratisravingother43lumb.
evidence.summary: Confirms the provider item, University of Toronto source, derivative inventory, absent item-level rights/license fields, and scan-leaf mappings. The separately cached scan data has SHA-256 a771c911d2bf3de4ef3b70539ab4f6ca2f03a8812c0812aad7a8ea9d38fa4dbc.

evidence.citation: R. Girvan, ed., Ratis Raving and Other Early Scots Poems on Morals, Scottish Text Society, 3rd series 11 (1939).
evidence.summary: Establishes the manuscript part's history and date, anonymous component creators, source relations, Girvan's use of a complete manuscript rotograph, rejection of Rolle and Foulis identifications, and complete later texts.
notes: The 336-page provider PDF is readable and target pages render. Non-fatal qpdf linearization warnings were recorded without modifying provider bytes.

evidence.citation: Helsinki Corpus of Older Scots bibliography entries SEDUC0A, SEDUC0C, and SEDUC0D.
evidence.summary: Confirms exact Girvan page-and-line ranges, corpus classifications and word counts, and that the 1460 date attached to Dicta is explicitly doubtful.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, unpublished description of Cambridge University Library MS Kk.1.5, transcribed by Martin Blake and published by CUL (2021).
evidence.summary: Official CUL publication confirming the composite host, separately covered part 6, physical description, and target loci ff. 1r-4r, 5r-12r, and 53r-54r.

evidence.citation: Dated search for a complete public CUL MS Kk.1.5 facsimile.
evidence.summary: No Kk.1.5 CUDL object was found. The deterministic shelfmark endpoint MS-KK-00001-00005 returned an error while adjacent MS-KK-00001-00006 resolved; domain searches found only the official Apollo description and scholarly index records.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Visiting Special Collections.
evidence.summary: Confirms reader registration, advance ordering, and onsite consultation routes for CUL special collections.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Image Supply and Reproduction.
evidence.summary: Confirms the official image-order form, private-study and publication routes, quotation, fees, file delivery, and need to identify the requested item and surfaces precisely.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Terms and Conditions - Image Supply and Reproduction.
evidence.summary: Establishes that newly supplied images and reuse permissions are governed by provider terms and the specific approved use.

evidence.citation: H. L. Pink, unpublished description of Cambridge University Library MS Ff.5.45, published by CUL (2021).
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete Ff.5.45 physical description and the fuller Middle English Craft of Dying analogue's folio range and structure.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library IIIF manifest for MS Ff.5.45.
evidence.locator: All 172 canvases; analogue at ff. 24v-46r / canvases 60-103
evidence.summary: Confirms a complete physical-object inventory and exact image sources. The cached bundle contains the provider manifest, inventory, and all 172 canvases; the cataloged RatisRav target works are absent.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 410411, The vertue of the masse, STC 17038.
evidence.summary: Confirms the circa-1520 Wynkyn de Worde imprint, Lydgate attribution, 24-page verse extent, Cambridge original, and subscription EEBO reproductions.
notes: The vendor-derived record is used only for bibliographic identification of an analogue, not to assert textual descent.

evidence.summary: Joint inspection of the XML title leaves and call number, Folger's 1969 record, Lumby's preface and texts, Girvan's codicology, and James's description supports the distinct artifact -> U-M encoding -> Greenwood 1969 -> Lumby 1870 -> King transcript -> Kk.1.5 part 6 chain.

evidence.citation: Lumby and Girvan's comparison evidence, checked against current CUL and Folger records.
evidence.summary: Supports Ff.5.45 and de Worde/Lydgate as scoped supporting analogues only. Neither is represented as a carrier, direct exemplar, or ancestor of the Kk.1.5 target works.

open_questions.question: Can a complete lawful digital facsimile of CUL MS Kk.1.5 be obtained?
next_steps: Request whole-object imaging through CUL, explicitly including all eight components, covers, endleaves, spine, and edges if complete physical coverage is required.
Recheck CUDL periodically without substituting selected target folios for the whole composite witness.

open_questions.question: Does W. W. King's complete working transcript of Kk.1.5 part 6 survive?
next_steps: Search Lumby, King, EETS, and CUL archival papers before treating the transcript as lost; preserve it as an unknown-survival intermediary meanwhile.

open_questions.question: What microfilm did U-M consult to place Dicta Salomonis folio milestones, and does it survive?
next_steps: Ask U-M HTI or CUL for legacy conversion records and microfilm identifiers; do not infer that the microfilm supplied the electronic wording.

open_questions.question: Does the complete Kk.1.5 part 6 rotograph used by Girvan survive under an identifiable holding?
next_steps: Search Girvan, Scottish Text Society, Glasgow, and CUL archival holdings if edition-production provenance becomes necessary.

open_questions.question: How should U-M's embedded 1997 redistribution restriction be reconciled with present repository distribution and any current U-M rights display?
next_steps: Seek source-specific clarification before commercial or content-provider redistribution; keep medieval text, print editions, digital markup, manuscript images, and repository files as separate rights layers.

review_notes: The cataloging boundary is exactly the three DIV1 prose works, not the broad source title, the eponymous Ratis Raving, the complete eleven-work manuscript part, or the complete print volume.
Current host and historical component are separate entities: Kk.1.5 is the composite holding object; part 6 was historically independent and remains separately covered.
The 1969 Greenwood carrier is the immediate print source of the U-M OCR; the 1870 Lumby edition is its reproduced predecessor, not silently the same physical edition.

King's working transcript, U-M's foliation microfilm, and Girvan's complete working rotograph are explicit intermediary source layers. The U-M microfilm was consulted only for milestone placement in Dicta Salomonis; Girvan transcribed through the separately modeled rotograph.

The Internet Archive PDF is complete as a 528-page compound provider object and contains Lumby's complete volume plus additional bound material; it is not described as a standalone 139-page edition scan.

The Ff.5.45 bundle is a complete supporting analogue cache with target_work_presence absent and no work_portion. Its ff. 24v-46r / canvases 60-103 range appears only in the analogue relation and notes.
Wynkyn de Worde/Lydgate material is comparison evidence only. Neither the early print nor Ff.5.45 is placed in the artifact's ancestry.
Public access, physical completeness, cached-file fixity, copyright status, licenses, and provider terms are separate assertions.
